entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: the word's own form is ascertained once the sound has illuminated it

entities.name: svarūpa
entities.cite: the word's own form is ascertained once the sound has illuminated it

entities.name: pratyaya
entities.cite: by cognitions that cannot be separately reported, yet are conducive to the final grasp

entities.name: grahaṇa
entities.cite: conducive to the final grasp, the word's own form is ascertained

entities.name: dhvani
entities.cite: once the sound has illuminated it

entities.name: avadharaṇa
entities.cite: it terminates in avadharana, the settled ascertainment of the word's svarupa

axioms.name: grahana-anuguna
axioms.cite: But they are grahana-anuguna, conducive to the grasp

axioms.name: terminates
axioms.cite: it terminates in avadharana, the settled ascertainment of the word's svarupa

axioms.name: ascertains
axioms.cite: the settled ascertainment of the word's svarupa

denials.name: summation
denials.cite: comprehension does in fact arrive as an all-at-once click after a subpersonal accumulation, not as a felt summation of parts

claims.name: anupakhyeya
claims.cite: by cognitions that cannot be separately reported

claims.name: grahana-anuguna
claims.cite: yet are conducive to the final grasp

claims.name: ascertains
claims.cite: the word's own form is ascertained

rejected_readings.label: composite_grasp
rejected_readings.rendering: The word's form is understood as a gradual accumulation of the meanings of its successive phonemes.
rejected_readings.why: Treats avadharana as a summation of individual pratyayas, which the commentary explicitly rejects. The unity of the object is surrendered, and the spontaneity of comprehension (the all-at-once click) is lost.

rejected_readings.label: reportable_cognitions
rejected_readings.rendering: Each intermediate stage in learning the word can be separately named and reported.
rejected_readings.why: Directly contradicts the commentary's characterization of intermediate cognitions as anupakhyeya. Sort error: treats pratyaya as explicitly reportable when the text insists they are not separately reportable.

notes: The contract encodes the sphota doctrine's phenomenology of learning: a gradual ripening through unreportable intermediate cognitions (pratyaya) that culminates in a sudden, unified grasp (avadharana) of the word's intrinsic form (svarupa). The sound (dhvani) illuminates but is not itself the object; the real work is subpersonal accumulation. This resolves the apparent paradox of gradual process yielding non-composite knowledge.
